title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After visiting a friend who is opening an auto repair shop, Bobby catches the 11:30 train from Paddington Station to return home. He narrowly boards the first-class carriage just as it departs and discovers the only other passenger is a dark-skinned girl named Frankie (Miss Francis DeWinter). The two share a history; their families, the Joneses and the DeWinters, were childhood acquaintances, though they have grown apart due to social differences and the formality of the Jones family.

During their journey, Frankie expresses exhaustion with her life in the countryside and recounts a recent, underwhelming social outing involving the Savoy Hotel and various venues like "Puppets on Strings" and "The Bullring." The conversation shifts to a recent news report regarding a "Fatal Accident in Sea Fog." Frankie reveals she read about the man who fell off a cliff, whom Bobby and Thomas had discovered. The newspaper identifies the deceased as Alexander Prichard, a man who had recently returned from Siam. Bobby notes that Prichard was not dead when they found him but died fifteen minutes later.

As they discuss the upcoming inquest in Marigold, Bobby mentions his friend Budge Bidden. Through a series of shared childhood memories regarding Budge's stuttering and past failures—including a bankrupt chicken farm and a brief stint at a stock brokerage—Frankie realizes she knows him, confirming his history of being sent to Australia before returning to England.
